vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The biome is in a state of severe degradation. P1 (String of Nickels), P2 (Mexican Mint), and P4 (Silver Guest) are not visually present as healthy specimens. P3 (Pothos) is represented only by the white rabbit anchor; the plant itself is absent or entirely obscured by soil/debris. The registry is largely invalidated by current physical reality.

plant_audit.P1: No visible foliage. Pot contains only dark, damp substrate.
plant_audit.P2: No visible foliage. Pot contains only dark, damp substrate.
plant_audit.P3: White rabbit anchor remains, but no Pothos foliage is detected. Substrate appears undisturbed.
plant_audit.P4: No visible foliage. Shared pot space is devoid of plant matter.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dark and damp across all pots, suggesting recent saturation or lack of evaporation.
biome_observations.surface_debris: Significant presence of crushed eggshells in the foreground pot. No fungal growth detected, but the lack of plant life suggests a dormant or failed biome.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Clean, with a white cable visible. No signs of pests or external contamination.

temporal_deltas.EARLIEST_to_T-4: Initial observation shows a single green sprout in the foreground pot.
temporal_deltas.T-4_to_T-3: Sprout remains stable; no significant growth or decline.
temporal_deltas.T-3_to_T-2: Sprout appears to be losing turgor pressure; slight drooping observed.
temporal_deltas.T-2_to_T-1: Sprout is no longer visible or has collapsed into the substrate.
temporal_deltas.T-1_to_CURRENT: No recovery. The biome remains devoid of active, visible plant life.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome has suffered a total collapse of the registered specimens. The single sprout observed in the early sequence failed to thrive and has likely perished or been buried. The environment is currently non-viable for the registered species.
anomalies: Presence of crushed eggshells (calcium amendment) in the foreground pot.
Total absence of expected foliage despite the presence of soil and pots.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a critical failure in the botanical environment. Starting from an initial state where a single, fragile sprout was present, the biome has regressed to a state of total plant loss. The white rabbit anchor in P3 serves as a stark reminder of the intended occupancy, now standing in an empty pot. The soil remains damp, but the lack of vegetative response indicates either seed failure, root rot, or total specimen expiration.
